a_image.alt: Botanical Theme style mood board — Natural · Plant Life

sections.a_eyebrow: Organic · Natural · Earthy · Warm · Green Tones
sections.a_description: Deep herbarium greens, aged cream paper, and the refined beauty of botanical illustration translated into digital form — Botanical Theme design brings the scholarly elegance of natural history collections to screen. The careful serif typography and warm ivory backgrounds create the feeling of browsing through a Victorian-era plant reference, where every leaf is both scientific specimen and object of beauty.

This nature-scholarly style powers plant nursery websites, botanical garden sites, herbal medicine companies, and garden design portfolios. You'll see it on seed catalog sites, landscape architecture firms, floral design studios, and nature journal publishers — anywhere that celebrates the intersection of scientific precision and natural beauty that has captivated botanists and gardeners for centuries.

a_image_cover.alt: Botanical Theme style use case illustration
Botanical Theme style use case illustration
Botanical Theme style use case illustration

sections.a_description: The elegant, botanical-illustration aesthetic — herbarium greens, vintage scientific illustration sensibility, and the refined beauty of plants as both subject and design language. Background: warm cream (#faf8f2) — the aged paper of a botanical journal. The .inner panel has an ivory background (#f9f6ee) with a 1px border (#d8cdb5) and 4px border-radius — like a plate in a botanical reference book. Colors: deep botanical green (#2d5a27) as the primary accent — the green of pressed leaves in an herbarium. Dark warm (#2a2218) for headings, olive gray (#6a6050) for body text. The aesthetic is scholarly-natural — the precision of scientific illustration meets the warmth of garden cultivation. Set in Libre Baskerville — a classic transitional serif with the authority of natural history publications. Heading at weight 700, 24px, line-height 1.3, color #2a2218. Eyebrow at weight 400, 11px, uppercase, 2px letter-spacing, color #2d5a27. Body at weight 400, 15px, line-height 1.8, color #6a6050. Buttons: primary — botanical green (#2d5a27), cream text (#faf8f2), 4px border-radius. Secondary: transparent, green border. Alternatives: Crimson Text (elegant reading), Spectral (refined screen serif).

items.a_h3: What AI prompt generates a Botanical Theme website?

Background: warm cream (#faf8f2) — the aged paper of a botanical journal. The .inner panel has an ivory background (#f9f6ee) with a 1px border (#d8cdb5) and 4px border-radius — like a plate in a botanical reference book.
